political.s: FEC: $16K PAC donations; 29% to Republican, 71% to Democratic committees.

charity.s: $12M reported corporate giving in 2024 (~0.06% of revenue).

wiki.extract: Safeway, Inc. is an American supermarket chain that provides grocery items and general merchandise along with various specialty departments such as bakery, delicatessen, floral, and pharmacy, as well as Starbucks coffee shops and vehicle fuel centers. Safeway's primary base of operations is in the Western United States, with some stores located in the Mid-Atlantic region of the Eastern Seaboard.

fec.committeeName: THE ALBERTSONS COMPANIES, INC. POLITICAL ACTION COMMITTEE (ACI PAC)

sec.note: Pay ratio extracted from proxy filing — verify manually for accuracy

health.s: 1 CPSC recall on record; sample hazard: electrical wiring in the lights can overheat.

topHazards.label: Electrical wiring in the lights can overheat, posing a fire hazard to consumers.

sampleRecalls.title: Holiday Lights Recalled by Four Star Group USA Due to Fire Hazard; Sold Exclusively at Safeway Stores
sampleRecalls.description: The recalled holiday lights are a string of 10 LED bulbs, each covered with a clear, star shape. "Fresh Décor" is printed on the front of the product's packaging; the UPC (#7989390761) is printed on the back, below the bar code.
hazards: Electrical wiring in the lights can overheat, posing a fire hazard to consumers.
injuries: The firm has received two reports of the recalled lights smoking and/or melting. No injuries have been reported.
products: Fresh Décor 10-Count LED Star String Lights

sampleRecords.event_description: Injured by slipping or swinging object held by injured worker
sampleRecords.final_narrative: An employee was getting the department set up for the day. He opened a package of frozen meat patties and began to pry them apart using a knife. The knife slipped and amputated the distal pad of his left middle finger.

sampleRecords.event_description: Other fall to lower level, unspecified
sampleRecords.final_narrative: An employee was standing on a conveyor's rollers watching a maintenance team work on a machine. The employee fell while trying to step down, landing on the concrete work surface and suffering a broken tibia and fibula in one leg.

sampleRecords.part_of_body: Back, including spine, spinal cord, unspecified
sampleRecords.nature: Third or fourth degree electrical burns
sampleRecords.event_description: Direct exposure to electricity, greater than 220 volts
sampleRecords.final_narrative: An employee was in an aerial lift when he contacted a 13.2 kV power line with his back. He was shocked and suffered second- and third-degree burns to the back.

sampleRecords.event_description: Caught in running equipment or machinery during regular operation
sampleRecords.final_narrative: An employee was operating the blow mold machine when the employee's fingers were caught in the machine, resulting in amputation of four fingers and a fractured arm.

sampleRecords.event_description: Other fall to lower level, unspecified
sampleRecords.final_narrative: An employee was climbing a scaffold ladder on a metal wall. When the ladder slid off, the employee fell and was impacted by the ladder, sustaining a broken left arm requiring surgery.
